body:
Cumulative fix branch for GS001 (NIST 800-53r5 page 28 / printed PAGE 1).

## Rounds in this PR

| round | row | route | landed in |
|---|---|---|---|
| R3 | runner contract gaps | review-extraction skill | f48c853 |
| R4 | row 2 (side DOI watermark) | nist_preset_ledger (text_classifier_rule + bbox_constraints) | 6fe6bee |
| R5 | row 3 (INTRODUCTION section heading) | pdf_oxide_core (narrow post-merge promotion hook) | 6fe6bee |
| R5 | row 4 (derMon artifact) | pdf_oxide_core (reproducer only — localized ownership) | 6fe6bee |
| R6 | row 4 (derMon fix) | pdf_oxide_core (sort spans by bbox.x inside classify_line) | 6fe6bee |
| R7 | extras triage (no code) | pdf_oxide_core / human_decision routes named | (bundle-only) |
| R8 | empty-block filter + bullet-list run grouping | pdf_oxide_core | 14ca00f |

## Test gates (all green)

- `tests/test_review_extraction_runner.py` — 21/21 PASS (R3 contract).
- `tests/test_nist_page_28_regression.py` — 6/6 PASS (R8, was 1/4 in R3):
  - `test_side_doi_chrome_routed_to_header_footer_noise` (R4)
  - `test_section_heading_INTRODUCTION_is_exact` (R5)
  - `test_no_element_text_contains_derMon_artifact` (R6)
  - `test_first_body_paragraph_starts_with_Modern_information_systems` (R6)
  - `test_no_release_element_has_empty_text` (R8)
  - `test_bullet_list_region_emits_as_one_logical_list` (R8)
- `cargo test block_classifier::tests` — 44/44 PASS incl. R6/R8 fixtures.

## Comparison summary

| metric | R3 | R4 | R5 | R6 | R8 |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| matched | 6 | 7 | 8 | 9 | **9** |
| missing | 3 | 2 | 1 | 0 | **0** |
| ambiguous | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | **0** |
| extras | 13 | 13 | 12 | 11 | **5** |
| pdf_oxide_core-blocking | — | — | — | 6 | **0** |
| human_decision items | — | — | — | 2 | **2** |
| accepted page chrome | — | — | — | 3 | **3** |

## R8 extras (5 remaining; 0 blocking)

| extra | type | decision | owner |
|---|---|---|---|
| block:1 (separator rule) | header_footer_noise | accepted_page_chrome_noise | no_change |
| block:4 (DOI watermark) | header_footer_noise | accepted_page_chrome_noise | no_change |
| block:5 (DOI watermark cont.) | header_footer_noise | accepted_page_chrome_noise | no_change |
| block:6 (CHAPTER ONE) | section_heading | chapter_label_expected_contract_decision | human_decision |
| block:8 (THE NEED TO PROTECT...) | paragraph_block | section_subtitle_expected_contract_decision | human_decision |

## Scope discipline (maintained across all 7 rounds)

- `classify_line` heading detection is NOT broadened (per WebGPT R5).
- No NIST text-anchor rules for body text (per WebGPT R6).
- `classify_blocks` Python binding is NOT changed (per WebGPT R6).
- Bullet-list grouping (R8) is narrow: only y_gap, font_size, indentation, and prior-block-type checks — does NOT consume footnotes/footer/captions.
- No mutations to closure matrix; no LLM calls; GS002 not started.

## Closure status

GS001 is at the **human_decision boundary**:

- 9/9 matched, 0 missing, 0 ambiguous.
- 0 pdf_oxide_core-blocking extras.
- 2 human_decision items awaiting expected_elements contract update:
  - block:6: should "CHAPTER ONE" be an expected row?
  - block:8: should the subtitle be an expected row, and what type?

Once those two decisions land (either add to expected_elements or explicitly waive), GS001 closes.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: GS001 R4-R6 cumulative: NIST 800-53r5 page 28 rows 2/3/4 + runner contract tests
